codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation of dashboard.json generation. Two new functions (_build_dashboard and _calculate_summary) added to pack_runner.py following existing helper patterns. Uses stdlib-only statistics module for mean/median. All 6 output files are generated correctly. Engagement/creator/media fields are promoted to top-level in dashboard samples. Summary aggregations (tag_frequency, score_distribution, engagement_range) are correctly computed with proper edge-case handling for empty inputs. 9 comprehensive tests added covering generation, structure, aggregation correctness, and cross-pack (streetwear) support. All 116 tests pass.

issues.description: datetime.now().isoformat() produces a timezone-naive timestamp. Consider using datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat() for explicitness, though the existing codebase also uses timezone-naive datetimes consistently.

The report's 'Runtime Check' block still says '("report files", "5")' but now 6 output files are generated. This is cosmetic since the report refers to 'report files' specifically, but updating to '6' would be more accurate.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md states 'Do NOT break existing 74 tests' but the test count is now 116 after multiple milestones (schema-and-engagement added many, plus 9 new dashboard tests). The number is stale.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md says 'Do NOT break existing 74 tests — they must pass at every commit' but python3 -m pytest tests/ -x -q shows '116 passed in 2.63s'

summary: PASS. The dashboard.json output feature is correctly implemented with clean code following project conventions (stdlib only, frozen dataclasses for schema, unittest.TestCase tests). Both _build_dashboard() and _calculate_summary() handle edge cases (empty samples). All 9 new tests pass alongside 107 existing tests (116 total). Both jade and streetwear packs produce correct dashboard.json with structured engagement/creator/media fields and accurate summary aggregations. Two minor non-blocking issues noted: timezone-naive datetime and stale report file count in the report's Runtime Check block.
